steps.title: Use the obstacle clue
steps.content: The phrase "every conceivable obstacle" emphasizes that nature seemed to put every possible obstacle in the travelers' path. In this context, "conceivable" describes what can be thought of or imagined.

steps.title: Match the context meaning
steps.content: A means clear or apparent, which is not the point. C means simple, and D means continuous or unbroken; both conflict with the idea of many obstacles. The word that means capable of being imagined matches the phrase. The answer is B.
